sections.title: What Changed This Week
sections.body: HealthKart launched 7 new video ads in 5 days — more than any other tracked brand — all centered on "Buy 2 Get 1 Free" discount framing, marking a shift from their prior educational content strategy. Mamaearth discontinued 4 long-running static ads (60+ days active) and replaced them with UGC-forward testimonial videos, signaling a full creative refresh. Oziva introduced their first carousel-format ad in 8 weeks, featuring a 5-step ingredient breakdown — a format no other supplement brand in the tracked set is currently using.

sections.title: Top Performing Formats
sections.body: Video ads account for 58% of all tracked ads and 71% of proven performers (30+ days active). Among video ads, the highest-longevity examples consistently open with a problem-statement hook — either a statistic or a relatable pain point framed as a question. Wow Skin Science's testimonial video has been running 52 days — the longest-active single creative in the personal care category.

sections.title: Emerging Message Themes
sections.body: Social proof is rapidly becoming the dominant messaging frame, rising from 18% to 27% of tracked ads in the past month — Mamaearth is leading this shift most aggressively. Concurrently, pure discount-led messaging has declined 12 percentage points among supplement brands, suggesting saturation. The most underused theme with high potential remains Education/Awareness in carousel format — only Oziva is currently experimenting in this space.

sections.title: Creative Gaps and Opportunities
sections.body: Three high-value gaps exist right now. First, carousel-format ingredient education is completely uncontested across personal care — BeBodywise could own this lane immediately. Second, no Man Matters competitor uses community-driven UGC video — the entire men's wellness space defaults to authority or performance messaging. Third: no brand competing with Little Joys runs a sustained educational video series on pediatric nutrition, which is emotionally high-value and competitively wide open.

sections.title: One Thing to Act on Immediately
sections.body: Launch a 5-part carousel series for BeBodywise with the frame "What your daily vitamin doesn't tell you." Each card covers one ingredient — biotin, iron, vitamin D3, folate, magnesium — with a clinical fact. This directly counters Oziva's emerging strategy before they scale it, occupies the most uncontested creative space in the category, and pairs clinical authority with an educational hook that is inherently shareable among health-conscious women 25–40.
